data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers.** Both `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  a = stackalloc nint[4];   // works
Span<nuint> b = stackalloc nuint[3];  // works
```

Under the hood, `nint` maps to `IntPtr` and `nuint` maps to `UIntPtr`. The test compiled and ran successfully on .NET 11 preview, producing correct values. This feature has been available since C# 9 / .NET 5 when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types.
